other_observers: at the time of sighting: Rich Levad and car-load of field trip participants. Feb 22: Bob Bradley.; I, Jason Beason, initially spotted and identified the bird. Rich Levad (next to me at the time) and Bob Bradley (found bird later) also saw this bird.
specific_location: About 1 mile north of O Rd on 19 Rd north-northeast of Fruita.
description: Chunky sparrow-sized bird darker on back and lighter on front. Chin area lighter than breast. Pointy beak appeared dark. Tail approximately medium length as far as sparrows go. Yellow spot on top front of head alerted observers that is was different species than White-crowned Sparrows it was associating with.Behaviors: Bird was discovered in a large flock of White-crowned Sparrows (WCSP). Habitat was brushy area (many shrubs in an area without overstory) along road. Bird perched up in Russian olive with other WCSP when flushed by our approaching car.Call: not heard at allPlumage: unknown
